name: Simplest Roast Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 cup chicken broth, water, fruit juice, or wine
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the oven rack on the second level from the bottom and preheat the oven to 500°F.
2. Remove excess fat from the tail and crop end of the chicken. Discard the neck and giblets or freeze them for making chicken stock later. Reserve the chicken livers for another use.
3. Stuff the cavity of the chicken with the lemon, garlic, and butter (if using). Season the cavity and skin with salt and pepper.
4. Place the chicken in a 12-by-8-by-1 ½-inch roasting pan, breast side up. Put the pan in the oven, legs first.
5. Roast for 50-60 minutes, or until juices run clear. After the first 10 minutes, gently move the chicken with a wooden spatula to prevent sticking.
6. Carefully remove the chicken from the roasting pan using a large wooden spoon and balance it with a kitchen spoon pressed against the crop end.
7. Tilt the chicken over the roasting pan so that all juices run out of the cavity into the pan. Discard any excess fat.
8. Transfer the pan to the stovetop, add the stock or other liquid, and bring to a boil. Scrape the bottom vigorously with a wooden spoon.
9. Reduce the sauce by half. Serve the sauce over the chicken or use it in a sauce boat for crisp skin.
